MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Joseph S. May, Esq. (415-781-3333) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Dean Pollack of Burnham Brown Law Offices (510-835-6705) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Transdev Services, Inc.

Attorney Sara Tobias of Archer Norris (925-930-6600) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant, Alofaga Tagaloa

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT TRANSDEV SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION on the issue of corporate punitive damages is DENIED. Triable issues of material fact exist, at a minimum, as to whether Mary McClain was a managing agent and whether she ratified Alofaga Tagaloa's alleged conduct. McClain oversees some 190 employees and has substantial independent authority over day-to-day operations of SF Paratransit. (PSS 8, 10, 37; White v. Ultramar, Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 563, 566-67.) There is also evidence that McClain failed to adequately investigate Tagaloa's alleged physical assault on plaintiff and/or to repudiate it and thus ratified his conduct. (PSS 38, 41-45; Herrick v. Quality Hotels, Inns and Resorts, Inc. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 1608, 1618; Greenfield v. Spectrum Investment Corp. (1985) 17 Cal.App3d 111, 121.) Indeed, Tagaloa was purportedly given a pay raise less than four months after assaulting plaintiff. (PSS 45.) Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Extending Fact Discovery Cutoff To Thirty Days Before The New Trial Date
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Brian Gearinger, Esq. of Gearinger Law Group (415-440-3175) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, Anthony Ghanayem

Attorney, Dean Pollack of Burnham Brown (510-444-6800) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Transdev Services Inc.

Attorney, Sarah E. Tobias of Archer Norris (925-930-6600) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Tagaloa

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides present, as set forth in the certified reporter's transcript. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT TRANSDEV SERVICES, INC'S. Motion For An Order Extending Fact Discovery Cutoff To Thirty Days Before The New Trial Date is GRANTED in part. In discovery, plaintiff notified defendant Transdev that a "mild brain injury" was claimed and, on June 21, 2016, invited a "physical and/or mental exam" of plaintiff - an invitation Transdev did not accept. However, plaintiff did not disclose until July 19, 2016 that a "permanent brain injury" was claimed, much less one with 16 psychological symptoms and damages in the "tens of millions." Even then, 23 days still remained before the August 12 discovery cutoff, but Transdev did not expeditiously seek to complete discovery and instead noticed this motion to be heard on August 15 - after the cutoff. I find the conduct of counsel for both sides to be wanting. The motion is GRANTED to the following limited extent and is otherwise DENIED: (1) a defense medical examination by a neuropsychiatrist may be conducted; the exam, including any testing, is limited to three hours; (2) plaintiff may be deposed regarding his alleged brain injury; the deposition is limited to two hours; (3) both the exam and the deposition must be completed by September 12, 2016; (4) neither the November 14, 2016, trial date, nor any other date, is hereby extended. There shall be one exam only, limited to 4 hours. The parties shall meet and confer regarding conditions and submit a proposed written order.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
